feed.title: alexandra edwards, phd
feed.subtitle: the internet homebase of dr. alexandra edwards

entry.title: Coherence, 2013 - ★★★
entry.content:
Watched on Thursday June 19, 2025.

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Mission: Impossible – The Final Reckoning, 2025 - ★★★
entry.content:
the future is unwritten !!

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Now You See Me 2, 2016 - ★★
entry.content:
meh

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Sinners, 2025 - ★★★★
entry.content:
from dusk till dawn meets idlewild?? with a dash of rosewood??????? fun!!!

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: very sad about the dallas stars
entry.content: this status update is echoed from bsky

entry.title: Dawson City: Frozen Time, 2016 - ★★★½
entry.content:
it takes a long while for the various threads to braid together, which makes the alaskan history section kind of a slog. I’m not sure about the choice to tell most of the story silently, nor the typographical choices tbh.

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Lost Forever: The Art of Film Preservation, 2011 - ★★★
entry.content:
short and sweet. I wish it was available in higher resolution—the version I watched on YouTube was in 480p :(

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Side by Side, 2012 - ★★★
entry.content:
a decent moment-in-time documentary about the overlapping eras of photochemical film and digital. would likely serve as a great introduction for students to the various material processes that filmmaking entails. keanu is a cutie.

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Splice Here: A Projected Odyssey, 2022 - ★★★★½
entry.content:
made me cry several times. i never knew where we were going next. energetic, thrilling even. i want to go see a film projected on film now.

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Thunderbolts*, 2025 - ★★★½
entry.content:
this was fine

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: We Own This City, 2022 - ★★★
entry.content:
i love it when a man does a little voice

(this was okay but it missed the mark in the end by clearly still believing in the project of policing and the promise of reform)

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Sharp Stick, 2022 - ★★
entry.content:
only here for one thing (jon bernthal’s tongue)

but this ended up being kind of cute and affirming ?

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Pilgrimage, 2017 - ★★
entry.content:
im choosing to view this as marvel canon

see this review on letterboxd.

entry.title: Text-to-Image Evolution: Computer Shrine
entry.content:
My ongoing exploration of AI image generation includes several experiments that help me understand the capabilities, limitations, and aesthetic tendencies of these systems. This note documents one such experiment: an iterative journey through different AI systems to develop the concept of a computer shrine in a garden setting.

The Evolution of a Digital Shrine

I began with a simple prompt in DeepAI: "Sci-fi Community Computer Garden." The result featured a vibrant, almost psychedelic garden with a vintage computer displaying a cosmic scene. What caught my attention was how the AI had placed the computer at the end of a stone path, giving it an almost shrine-like quality.

Intrigued by this shrine-like interpretation, I refined my prompt to explicitly request "a radiant computer shrine" while adding elements like "moss-covered stones," "dappled sunlight," and "cute anime style." DeepAI responded by generating an image heavily influenced by Studio Ghibli aesthetics, featuring a traditional Japanese shrine at the end of a stone path. While aesthetically pleasing, I found this image to be boring and too derivative.

For my third attempt, I kept the core concept but added more technological elements and specified a different artistic style: "A computer shrine powered by solar panels... whimsical solarpunk garden, linocut." This resulted in a dramatic stylistic shift—a minimalist, graphic illustration reminiscent of linocut prints, featuring a vintage computer beneath a solar panel.

The image includes a generated nonsense label or title and an approximation of a copyright mark. This suggests that the AI is attempting to create a more 'authentic' artifact, but it also points to the likelihood of DeepAI's training data including copyrighted material.

For the final iteration, I fed the third image into Adobe's Text-to-Image generator. The result was a synthesis and evolution—maintaining the core elements (computer, solar panel, garden path) while developing a more sophisticated aesthetic with detailed texturing, retrofuturist styling, and a cosmic portal displayed on the screen. The flowers became more prominent, creating a striking color contrast with the predominantly blue-green scene.

Aesthetic Evolution and Observations

This experiment reveals several interesting aspects of AI image generation:

  1. Aesthetic Progression: The images move from fantasy-like saturation to traditional serenity to graphic minimalism to detailed retrofuturism—demonstrating how different systems interpret similar concepts through distinct visual languages.

  2. Symbolic Consistency: The stone pathway remains a consistent compositional element across all iterations, though its rendering evolves in the final iteration.

  3. Concept Transformation: The representation of technology shifts from literal (vintage computer) to metaphorical (shrine) and back to literal but progressively more abstract and cosmic.

  4. Color Evolution: The color palettes vary significantly—from vibrant complementary colors in the first image, to natural greens and yellows in the second, to restrained black and white in the third, to rich blues and oranges with textural depth in the fourth.

Reflections on the Process

For me, this experiment demonstrates how AI image generation can be used in a critical making process, with each output informing subsequent prompts and systems. It reveals both the constraints of these systems (how they interpret and sometimes transform conceptual elements into derivative depictions) and their creative potential when used in an exploratory fashion.

Given the likelihood of DeepAI's training data including copyrighted material, I find myself unlikely to use it for "final" versions of images. I feel more comfortable using Adobe's system, which has been trained on their stock image library, suggesting artists have been at least somewhat compensated for their work.

The next step in this experiment will likely involve using the final generated image as a "prompt" for my own drawing or painting, allowing me to explore the intersection of AI-generated imagery and human creativity. This could lead to a more nuanced understanding of how these systems can inform and inspire traditional artistic practices.

Questions for Further Exploration

System Boundaries and Interpretations

  • How do different AI systems prioritize elements in complex prompts?
  • When does a system choose to transform a concept rather than represent it literally?
  • How do aesthetics from different traditions get encoded in and reproduced by these systems?

Process and Methodology

  • What are the advantages of iterative approaches to AI image generation?
  • How might using multiple systems in sequence enhance creative possibilities?
  • What is lost and gained as images move between systems and prompts?

This exploration connects to my broader investigations of AI aesthetics and solarpunk as pedagogy.

entry.title: building shortcuts on the couch
entry.content: this status update is echoed from bsky

entry.title: Site Documentation
entry.content:
This document contains essential information for maintaining and developing my personal site built with Zola and the Artemis theme.

Common Commands

# Start development server with 
zola serve --drafts

# Build site for production
# usually not needed since GitHub Actions handles this
# but useful for local testing
zola build

Frontmatter Templates

Standard Note Template

+++
title = "Note Title"
date = "2025-05-02"
draft = false
taxonomies:
  tags = ["tag1", "tag2"]
extra:
  moon_phase = "waxing_crescent" # Options: new, waxing_crescent, first_quarter, waxing_gibbous, full
  tldr = "A brief summary of this note's content."
  related_notes_links = [
    {
      "url": "/notes/related-note/",
      "title": "Related Note Title",
      "description": "Brief description of the related note",
      "moon_phase": "first_quarter"
    }
  ]
+++

Standard Page Template

+++
title = "Page Title"
date = "2025-05-02"
insert_anchor_links = "heading"
+++

Moon Phase Progression System

For a basic reference, see the table below. For complete design guidelines, see the Digital Moon Garden Style Guide.

PhaseEmojiClassMeaning
New Moon🌑moon-phase-newBrand new ideas, initial thoughts
Waxing Crescent🌙moon-phase-waxing_crescentBeginning to take shape
First Quarter🌓moon-phase-first_quarterHalf-developed
Waxing Gibbous🌔moon-phase-waxing_gibbousMostly formed
Full Moon🌕moon-phase-fullComplete thought

Shortcodes and Snippets

See the Shortcode Examples for quick reference and the Lunar Style Guide for more information on divider styles.

Valid OG Emojis

For a complete list of emojis that work with the og class, see the Lunar Style Guide.

Site Structure

content/            
├── _index.md    # Homepage
├── is/          # Status updates echoed from Bluesky
├── log/         # Movie log echoed from Letterboxd
├── meta/        # Site documentation
├── notes/       # Digital garden notes
├── reading/     # Reading list
├── research/    # CV
└── teaching/    # Index of courses


themes/apollo/      
├── templates/      # HTML templates
├── sass/           # Styling
└── static/         # Static assets
    └── images/     # Images

Troubleshooting

  • If styles aren't updating, try clearing your browser cache
  • For template changes, restart the Zola server
  • Taxonomy issues often require rebuilding the site with zola build
  • For deployment issues, check GitHub Actions logs

Related Documentation

  • Zola Documentation
  • Moon Style Guide

entry.title: OG Emoji Reference Table
entry.content:
Credit

OG Emoji font created by Monica Dinculescu.
EmojiDescriptionAdditional Uses/Meanings
❤HeartLove, favorite, health, romance
💔Broken heartHeartbreak, loss, disappointment, rejection
💓Beating heartEmotion, pulse, life, vitality
💕Two heartsDeep affection, relationship, partnership
😃Happy faceJoy, positivity, greeting, good news
😖Worried faceStress, anxiety, confusion, distress
😞Sad faceDisappointment, regret, unhappiness
😵Dizzy faceOverwhelmed, shocked, stunned
😠Angry faceFrustration, annoyance, displeasure
🎵Musical noteSong, audio, melody, entertainment
♨Hot springsWarmth, relaxation, thermal features, spa
💠Diamond shapePrecious, valuable, highlight, achievement
💋Kiss markAffection, romance, farewell, appreciation
✨SparklesMagic, special, new, clean, highlight
💡Light bulbIdea, insight, inspiration, solution
💢Anger symbolIrritation, frustration, conflict
👊FistSolidarity, strength, resistance, bump
💣BombExplosive, disaster, big impact, problem
🎶Musical notesSong, soundtrack, melody, audio content
💤Zzz (sleep)Rest, dreaming, tired, inactive
❗ExclamationImportant, alert, warning, emphasis
⁉Exclamation questionSurprise, disbelief, confusion
‼Double exclamationExtra emphasis, shock, urgency
💥CollisionImpact, crash, explosion, conflict
💦Sweat dropsEffort, nervousness, hot, working hard
💧DropletWater, tear, sadness, hydration
💨Dash symbolSpeed, wind, departure, running away
〰️Wavy dashVibration, approximately, uncertainty
➰Curly loopContinuity, cycle, repetition
⤴Up-right arrowDirection, increase, progress
⤵Down-right arrowDecline, download, descending
↗Up-right arrowGrowth, positive trend, northeast
↘Down-right arrowDescent, negative trend, southeast
↖Up-left arrowNorthwest direction, back and up
↙Down-left arrowSouthwest direction, back and down
☀SunSunshine, daylight, warmth, happiness
☁CloudOvercast, dream, thought, storage
☂Umbrella with rainProtection, precipitation, shelter
☃SnowmanWinter, cold, holiday season, fun
⚡︎LightningEnergy, power, electricity, speed
🌀CycloneStorm, spiral, chaos, dizziness
🌁FoggyUnclear, mysterious, hazy, uncertainty
🌂Closed umbrellaProtection, preparedness, compact
♈︎AriesZodiac sign, fire element, March-April
♉︎TaurusZodiac sign, earth element, April-May
♊︎GeminiZodiac sign, air element, May-June
♋︎CancerZodiac sign, water element, June-July
♌︎LeoZodiac sign, fire element, July-August
♍︎VirgoZodiac sign, earth element, August-September
♎︎LibraZodiac sign, air element, September-October
♏︎ScorpioZodiac sign, water element, October-November
♐︎SagittariusZodiac sign, fire element, November-December
♑︎CapricornZodiac sign, earth element, December-January
♒︎AquariusZodiac sign, air element, January-February
♓︎PiscesZodiac sign, water element, February-March
🎽Running shirtExercise, marathon, fitness, sports
⚾︎BaseballSports, game, play, summer activity
⛳Golf flagTarget, achievement, sports, recreation
🎾TennisSport, game, leisure, court
⚽︎Soccer ballFootball, sports, game, international
🎿SkisWinter sports, snow, vacation, mountain
🏀BasketballSports, game, recreation, competition
🏁Checkered flagFinish line, completion, racing
📟PagerCommunication, retro technology, notification
🚃Railway carTransportation, travel, commute, journey
ⓂMetroSubway, transportation, urban travel
🚄High-speed trainFast travel, modernity, efficiency
🚗AutomobileTransportation, driving, travel, commute
🚙SUVVehicle, outdoors, family car, utility
🚌BusPublic transportation, school, commute
🚢ShipTravel, cruise, vacation, maritime
✈AirplaneTravel, flight, vacation, departure
🏠HouseHome, residence, domestic, shelter
🏢Office buildingWork, business, corporate, urban
🏣Japanese post officeMail, communication, Japan
🏥HospitalHealthcare, medical, emergency, wellness
🏦BankMoney, finance, savings, economy
🏧ATMCash, banking, withdrawal, finance
🏨HotelAccommodation, travel, stay, vacation
🏪Convenience storeShopping, retail, quick purchases
⛽Fuel pumpGas, energy, refill, road trip
🅿ParkingCar park, vehicle storage, urban facility
🚥Traffic lightRegulation, stop/go, urban infrastructure
🚻RestroomBathroom, facilities, public space
🍴Fork and knifeDining, meal, restaurant, food
☕CoffeeBeverage, caffeine, morning, energy
🍸Cocktail glassDrink, alcohol, celebration, social
🍺Beer mugDrink, alcohol, relaxation, celebration
🍔HamburgerFood, fast food, meal, American cuisine
👠High-heeled shoeFashion, formal, femininity, elegance
✂ScissorsCutting, crafts, editing, tool
🎤MicrophoneSinging, speech, performance, audio
🎥Movie cameraFilm, recording, cinema, video
🎠Carousel horseAmusement park, childhood, fun, ride
🎧HeadphoneMusic, audio, listening, privacy
🎨Artist paletteCreativity, art, design, colors
🎩Top hatFormal, elegance, magic, gentleman
🎪Circus tentEntertainment, performance, spectacle
🎫TicketEvent, admission, entertainment, access
🚬CigaretteSmoking, tobacco, addiction
🚭No smokingProhibition, health, regulation
📷CameraPhotography, image, memory, visual
👜HandbagFashion, accessory, storage, style
📖Open bookReading, knowledge, literature, learning
🎀RibbonGift, decoration, prize, femininity
🎁Wrapped giftPresent, celebration, generosity
🎂Birthday cakeCelebration, anniversary, party
☎︎TelephoneCommunication, call, contact
📱Mobile phoneCommunication, technology, connectivity
📝MemoNotes, writing, documentation, reminder
📺TelevisionEntertainment, media, broadcast, screen
🎮Video gameGaming, entertainment, digital, play
💿Optical discData, music, storage, media
♥Heart suitCards, love, games, gambling
♠Spade suitCards, games, gambling, luck
♦Diamond suitCards, games, gambling, treasure
♣Club suitCards, games, gambling, luck
👀EyesLooking, watching, attention, witness
👂EarListening, hearing, attention, sound
✊Raised fistSolidarity, resistance, strength, power
✌Victory handPeace, success, triumph, posing
✋Raised handStop, high five, greeting, volunteer
👣FootprintsJourney, path, progress, tracking
👟Athletic shoeSports, running, casual, footwear
👓GlassesVision, reading, intelligence, clarity
🌑New moonNew beginnings, darkness, cycle start
🌔Waxing gibbous moonGrowth, progress, almost complete
🌓First quarter moonBalance, half-way point, progress
🌙Crescent moonNight, Islam, sleep, dreams
🌕Full moonCompletion, illumination, maximum
🐶DogPet, loyalty, friendship, playfulness
🐱CatPet, independence, mystery, comfort
⛵SailboatVoyage, leisure, water travel, adventure
🎄Christmas treeHoliday, winter, celebration, tradition
📲Mobile phone with arrowIncoming call, messaging, contact
📩Envelope with arrowEmail, message, correspondence
📠Fax machineOffice, communication, document transfer
✉︎EnvelopeMail, message, letter, correspondence
💴Yen banknoteMoney, currency, Japan, finance
🆓Free buttonNo cost, available, accessible
🆔ID buttonIdentification, proof, verification
↩Right arrow curving leftReply, return, backward, undo
🆑CL buttonClear, cancel, reset, Japanese symbol
🔍Magnifying glassSearch, investigation, detail, lookup
🆕New buttonRecent, update, fresh, novelty
🚩Triangular flagWarning, milestone, location marker
➿Double curly loopInfinity, repetition, forever, cycle
#Number signHashtag, pound, number, social media tag
0Keycap 0Number, digit, zero, count
1Keycap 1Number, digit, first, primary
2Keycap 2Number, digit, second, pair
3Keycap 3Number, digit, third, triplet
4Keycap 4Number, digit, fourth, quartet
5Keycap 5Number, digit, fifth, quintet
6Keycap 6Number, digit, sixth, half dozen
7Keycap 7Number, digit, seventh, luck
8Keycap 8Number, digit, eighth, octave
9Keycap 9Number, digit, ninth, final digit
🆗OK buttonApproval, agreement, confirmation

entry.title: Shortcode Examples
entry.content:
This guide demonstrates how to use the shortcodes and other visual elements available.

Divider Shortcode

The divider shortcode creates decorative separators with various moon-themed designs.

Code:

  • Default: {{ divider() }}
  • Line: {{ divider(style="line") }}
  • New Moon: {{ divider(style="new_moon") }}
  • Simple: {{ divider(style="simple") }}
  • Quarter: {{ divider(style="quarter") }}
  • Ornate: {{ divider(style="ornate") }}

Divider Styles

Default/moon phases divider:

Line/stars divider:

New moon divider:

Simple/crescent moon divider:

Quarter moon divider:

Ornate/full moon divider:

Working with OG Emoji

The theme includes a special vintage emoji font. To use it, wrap emojis in <span class="og"> tags:

Code:

<span class="og">📷</span> This text has a vintage camera emoji

Output: A vintage-styled camera emoji followed by regular text

For a complete list of emojis that work with the og class, see the Emoji Table.

Setting Moon Phases in Content

To indicate a note's development stage, add moon phase information to the frontmatter:

+++
title = "My Note Title"
date = "2025-05-01"
[extra]
moon_phase = "waxing_crescent"
+++

Valid moon phase values:

  • new (🌑)
  • waxing_crescent (🌙)
  • first_quarter (🌓)
  • waxing_gibbous (🌔)
  • full (🌕)

Adding Related Notes

To display related notes at the bottom of a page, add this to the frontmatter:

[extra]
related_notes_links = [
  {
    "url": "/notes/example/",
    "title": "Example Note",
    "description": "Description of the related note",
    "moon_phase": "waxing_crescent"
  },
  {
    "url": "/notes/another-example/",
    "title": "Another Example",
    "description": "Description of another related note",
    "moon_phase": "full"
  }
]

This will automatically generate a "Related Notes" section with links and descriptions.

Adding a TL;DR Summary

To add a summary box at the top of your content:

[extra]
tldr = "A brief summary of this note's content."

This will create a highlighted summary box below the title.

HTML Snippets

While not shortcodes, use these HTML snippets for consistent formatting:

Callouts

Callouts are styled boxes for highlighting important information.

Available styles:

info

Used for informational content, definitions, or reference material.

alert

Used for critical information or urgent messages.

success

Used for successful outcomes or proven methods.

warning

Used for cautionary notes or potential issues.

Image Formatting

Use the following HTML snippets to format images with captions and pixel corners.

Centered Image with Caption

<figure class="center">
  <div class="pixel-corners--wrapper">
    <img src="/path/to/image.jpg" alt="Description" width="450px">
  </div>
  <figcaption>Caption text</figcaption>
</figure>

Floating Right Image

<figure class="float-right">
  <div class="pixel-corners--wrapper">
    <img src="/path/to/image.jpg" alt="Description" width="450px">
  </div>
  <figcaption>Caption text</figcaption>
</figure>

Floating Left Image

<figure class="float-left">
  <div class="pixel-corners--wrapper">
    <img src="/path/to/image.jpg" alt="Description" width="450px">
  </div>
  <figcaption>Caption text</figcaption>
</figure>

Pixel Corners (without figure)

<div class="pixel-corners--wrapper">
  <img src="/path/to/image.jpg" alt="Description" width="100%">
</div>

entry.title: Lunar Style Reference Sheet
entry.content:
This guide provides a quick reference for applying the lunar-themed design system to this digital garden.

Growth Stages & Moon Phases

StageMoonClassMeaningStyling
New Moon🌑.moon-phase-newBrand new ideas, questions, or thoughts
Waxing Crescent🌒.moon-phase-waxing_crescentGrowing ideas with some development
First Quarter🌓.moon-phase-first_quarterHalf-developed notes with structure
Waxing Gibbous🌔.moon-phase-waxing_gibbousNearly complete content
Full Moon🌕.moon-phase-fullComplete, mature notes

Dividers by Complexity

ComplexitySymbolUsage
Line⋆ ✧ ⋆ ✧ ⋆All-purpose
New Moon🌑For new-moon and early-stage content, towards the beginning of documents
Simple☽For crescent moon ideas
Quarter🌓 🌓 🌓For half-formed content and the halfway mark of documents
Default🌑 ☽ 🌓 For nearly complete content, towards the end of documents
Ornate✨ 🌕 ✨For full moon notes and the final divider of a document, or to mark a major section break

Line/stars divider:

New moon divider:

Simple/crescent moon divider:

Quarter moon divider:

Moon phases divider:

Ornate/full moon divider:

Special Indicators

SymbolMeaningUsage
⭐Starred contentImportant or featured notes
✨Connected ideasNotes with multiple connections

Color System

Base Colors

  • Primary: #FFC107 (Amber)
  • Primary Light: #FFECB3 (Light amber)
  • Primary Dark: #FF8F00 (Dark amber)
  • Background: #fff (Light background)
  • Text: #212121 (Dark text)

Callout Colors & Usage

  • Info (Blue): #007FFF - Reference material, definitions
  • Alert (Red): #fb706e - Critical information, blockers
  • Success (Green): #AAD6A0 - Completed work, proven methods
  • Warning (Orange): #fcb388 - Considerations, potential issues

entry.title: happily grinding away in the webdev mines
